threats.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
threats.summary: WhatsApp on Monday officially announced the start of global reservations of usernames with an aim to protect the privacy of more than three billion users on the messaging platform. The optional feature is designed to help users connect with someone on the service through usernames, as opposed to directly sharing their phone numbers. Username reservations will start rolling out starting today,

threats.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
threats.summary: Today’s encrypted data, such as credentials, may no longer remain confidential in the future because the public-key cryptography protecting it will soon be broken by quantum computers.
